data.title: test-index: stale RED markers accumulate for tests that have gone GREEN -> record-test-status.sh blocks on stale-red-marker
data.description:
## Incident Overview
During this session, record-test-status.sh reported 'failed — stale-red-marker' twice for tests that pass consistently:
1. test-record-review-attest.sh [test_attest_writes_passed_status] — test passes, marker stale
2. test-harvest-worktree.sh [test_clean_merge_produces_commit] — test passes, marker stale

Each time, the workaround was to manually edit .test-index (grep -v the marker line), re-stage, and re-run record-test-status.sh --restart.

## Expected Behavior
When a RED-marked test transitions to GREEN (the feature was implemented and the test passes), the RED marker should be automatically removed from .test-index — either by record-test-status.sh itself, or by the sprint/fix-bug workflow that implements the feature.

## Actual Behavior
RED markers persist indefinitely after the feature goes GREEN. They silently block the test gate on subsequent commits that touch the same source file, requiring manual intervention.

## Root Cause Hypothesis
The sprint/fix-bug workflow writes RED markers during Step 5 (RED test) but does not remove them after Step 7 (verify GREEN). The markers are left for the next session to discover.
